concept:
AGL - Above Ground Level
DME - Distance Measuring Equipment
FAA - Federal Aviation Administration
FAR - Federal Aviation Regulations
IFR - Instrument Flight Rules
IMC - Instrument Meterological Conditions
KIAS - Knots Indicated Air Speed
MSL - Mean Sea Level
NDB - Non Directional Beacon
NTSB - National Transportation Safety Board
PIC - Pilot in command
VFR - Visual Flight Rules
VOR - VHF Omnidirectional Range
"FAA" stands for: Federal Aviation Administration Federal Aviation Association Federal Airplane Administration Flying Aviation Administration
"FAR" stands for: Federal Aviation Regulations Flying Answers and Rules Federal Aviation Rules Flying Aviation Regulations
"IMC" stands for: Instrument Meterological Conditions Instrument Measured Conditions Infrared Metered Changes International Measured Conditions
"IFR" stands for: Instrument Flight Rules International Flying Regulations Internal Flying Rules Instrument Flight Routes
"VFR" stands for: Visual Flight Rules Virtual Flying Regulations Varied Flying Rules Valid Flight Routes
"NDB" stands for: Non Directional Beacon Node Directed Broadcast Nodal Diverse Beacon Non Diverse Broadcast
"VOR" stands for: VHF Omnidirectional Range Vector Oriented Radio Vector Offset Radio VHF Offset Radio
"DME" stands for: Distance Measuring Equipment Distance Monitored Elapsed Data Measured Enroute Data Mileage Equipment
"NTSB" stands for: National Transportation Safety Board Network of Transport Safety Boards National Transcontinental Safety Board North-Atlantic Transcontinental Safety Boards
"PIC" stands for: Pilot in Command Person in Control Pilot in Control Person in Command
"KIAS" stands for: Knots Indicated Air Speed Known Indexed Altitude/Speed Known Indicated Altitude Safety Knots Indexed Air Speed
"AGL" stands for: Above Ground Level Around Ground Limit Above Governance Limit Around Governance Level
"MSL" stands for: Mean Sea Level Mid Sea Limit Mean System Limit Mid System Level
